fault_hypothesis: Lines 73-74 incorrectly pop the last element when both streams end with the same value, but this is wrong — both copies should be preserved. The condition on line 73 causes an erroneous removal of a valid duplicate.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid merged output — if it fires, a valid duplicate will be wrongly removed.
After initialization merged__1 must be empty before any merging starts.
After each loop iteration, the number of merged elements must equal the sum of elements consumed from both streams.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as both input streams combined.
